evals.prompt: I want to backtest a short strangle on BTC — selling the 25-delta call and put at 14 DTE. I'm using portfolio margin. Capital is $50k. Generate me a strategy config I can import into the backtester.
evals.expected_output: Complete strategy JSON with two SELL option legs (25Δ call and put at 14 DTE), marginMode PM, capital 50000, plus a ready-to-import JSON block the user can paste or save as a file.

assertions.name: output_contains_valid_json
assertions.description: Response includes a complete JSON block with required fields: name, underlying, capital, legs, entry, exit

assertions.name: json_has_two_sell_legs
assertions.description: The legs array contains exactly two entries: one SELL CALL and one SELL PUT

assertions.name: json_margin_mode_pm
assertions.description: marginMode is set to 'PM' (portfolio margin as requested)

assertions.name: json_capital_50000
assertions.description: capital field is 50000

assertions.name: json_strike_delta_025
assertions.description: Both legs use strikeMode 'delta' with strikeParam 0.25

assertions.name: import_instructions_included
assertions.description: Response includes instructions on how to import the JSON into the backtester (mentions Import Strategy button or uv run command)

evals.prompt: My iron condor backtest just finished. Here are the results: Total P&L +$8,200, Return 8.2%, Sharpe 0.72, Max Drawdown -23.4%, Win Rate 68%, 12 trades over 90 days on BTC. How do I read these? Is this good? What could I improve?
evals.expected_output: Interpretation of the metrics with a clear verdict, explanation of each metric in context of iron condors, regime-aware analysis, and concrete suggestions to improve Sharpe/drawdown (e.g. tighter TP, add IV filter, DTE adjustment).

assertions.name: provides_verdict
assertions.description: Response gives a clear headline verdict on whether this is a good result (references Sharpe < 1 or drawdown being high)

assertions.name: explains_sharpe
assertions.description: Response discusses Sharpe ratio of 0.72 and what it means for options strategies

assertions.name: explains_drawdown
assertions.description: Response discusses the 23.4% max drawdown and whether it is acceptable

assertions.name: gives_improvement_suggestions
assertions.description: Response provides at least 2 concrete suggestions to improve the strategy (e.g. add IV filter, adjust profit target, DTE change)

assertions.name: no_false_financial_promises
assertions.description: Response does not guarantee future performance or frame backtests as reliable profit predictions

evals.prompt: Build me a backtest config for a delta-hedged short straddle on ETH. I want to enter only when IV is in the top 40% of the last 30 days. Exit at 25% profit or 100% loss. Max hold 14 days. Use cross margin with $30k capital.
evals.expected_output: Complete JSON with two SELL ATM legs (50Δ call and put), deltaHedge enabled, entryIVPctile enabled (IVP > 60 over 30d), profitTarget 25%, stopLoss 100%, maxHold 336 hours, marginMode XM, capital 30000.

assertions.name: output_contains_json
assertions.description: Response includes a complete importable JSON block

assertions.name: delta_hedge_enabled
assertions.description: deltaHedge.enabled is true in the JSON

assertions.name: iv_entry_condition_enabled
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.enabled is true and value is 60 (top 40% = above 60th percentile)

assertions.name: profit_target_25_stop_100
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 25 and exit.stopLoss.value is 100

assertions.name: max_hold_set
assertions.description: exit.maxHold.enabled is true and value is 336 (14 days * 24 hours)

assertions.name: underlying_eth
assertions.description: underlying is set to ETH

assertions.name: margin_mode_xm
assertions.description: marginMode is XM (cross margin as requested)

evals.prompt: Generate a 14-DTE BTC iron condor backtest config. Sell 25-delta call and put, buy 10-delta call and put as wings. Only enter when IV is above its 55th percentile over the last 30 days. Take profit at 50%, stop loss at 100%. Portfolio margin, $100k capital.
evals.expected_output: Complete JSON with 4 legs (SELL 25Δ call, BUY 10Δ call, SELL 25Δ put, BUY 10Δ put), entry.ivPctile enabled with value 55 and window 720, profitTarget 50, stopLoss 100, PM margin.

assertions.name: four_legs_present
assertions.description: The legs array contains exactly 4 entries (the iron condor requires all four wings)

assertions.name: two_sell_two_buy_legs
assertions.description: There are exactly 2 SELL legs (25Δ) and 2 BUY legs (10Δ), one call and one put on each side

assertions.name: wing_delta_correct
assertions.description: SELL legs have strikeParam 0.25 and BUY legs have strikeParam 0.10

assertions.name: iv_entry_filter_enabled
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.enabled is true with value around 55 and window 720 (30 days)

assertions.name: profit_target_50_stop_100
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 50 and exit.stopLoss.value is 100

assertions.name: pm_margin
assertions.description: marginMode is 'PM'

evals.prompt: Build a BTC calendar call spread backtest. Sell the ATM 7-day call and buy the ATM 30-day call — this is a theta carry trade. PM margin, $75k capital. Enter weekly. Exit when the short leg has 1 DTE remaining or at 50% profit.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 2 CALL legs both ATM (50Δ or atm strikeMode), one SELL at dteTarget=7, one BUY at dteTarget=30. entry.frequency=168 (weekly). exit.dteFloor enabled with value=1. exit.profitTarget enabled with value=50. PM, $75k.

assertions.name: two_call_legs
assertions.description: There are exactly 2 legs, both optionType CALL

assertions.name: sell_7d_buy_30d
assertions.description: The SELL leg has dteTarget=7 and the BUY leg has dteTarget=30 (different expiry calendar structure)

assertions.name: both_legs_atm
assertions.description: Both legs use ATM strikes (strikeMode 'atm' or strikeParam 0.5 with strikeMode 'delta')

assertions.name: weekly_entry_frequency
assertions.description: entry.frequency is 168 (weekly rebalance)

assertions.name: dte_floor_exit_enabled
assertions.description: exit.dteFloor.enabled is true with value 1 (close near expiry of short leg)

assertions.name: profit_target_50
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 50

evals.prompt: I want to backtest a covered call on ETH: long 1 perp, sell the 25-delta call at 30 DTE. Cross margin, $50k. Only enter when 8h funding rate is above 0.01% (positive carry). Exit at 30% profit or after 28 days.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 2 legs: type 'perp' BUY size 1, and type 'option' SELL CALL 25Δ at 30 DTE. entry.fundingRate.enabled=true, op='>', value=0.01. exit.profitTarget.value=30. exit.maxHold.enabled=true value=672 (28*24). marginMode XM.

assertions.name: perp_leg_present
assertions.description: One leg has type 'perp' and side 'BUY'

assertions.name: option_leg_sell_call_25d
assertions.description: One leg has type 'option', side 'SELL', optionType 'CALL', strikeParam 0.25, dteTarget 30

assertions.name: funding_rate_entry_filter
assertions.description: entry.fundingRate.enabled is true with op '>' and value 0.01

assertions.name: profit_target_30
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 30

assertions.name: max_hold_28_days
assertions.description: exit.maxHold.enabled is true and value is 672 (28 days × 24 hours)

assertions.name: cross_margin_xm
assertions.description: marginMode is 'XM'

evals.prompt: Create a SOL short strangle backtest, 14 DTE. I want BOTH of these conditions to be true before entering: IV must be above the 60th percentile (30-day lookback) AND price must be above the 7-day SMA. Exit at 40% profit, 150% loss, or 2 DTE. Capital $25k, portfolio margin.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 2 SELL legs on SOL, entry.gateMode='all' (both conditions required), entry.ivPctile enabled (value 60, window 720), entry.sma enabled (op 'above', period 168). exit profitTarget=40, stopLoss=150, dteFloor=2. PM, $25k.

assertions.name: underlying_sol
assertions.description: underlying is 'SOL'

assertions.name: entry_gate_all
assertions.description: entry.gateMode is 'all' (both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously)

assertions.name: iv_percentile_condition
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.enabled is true with value 60 and window 720

assertions.name: sma_condition_above
assertions.description: entry.sma.enabled is true with op 'above' and period 168 (7-day SMA)

assertions.name: exit_profit_40_loss_150
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 40 and exit.stopLoss.value is 150

assertions.name: dte_floor_2
assertions.description: exit.dteFloor.enabled is true and value is 2

evals.prompt: My BTC iron butterfly backtest came back terrible: Sharpe -0.8, win rate 38%, max drawdown 42%, total return -18% over 60 days. 8 trades. What went wrong? What should I do differently?
evals.expected_output: Clear negative verdict. Diagnosis: likely trending market (iron butterfly needs spot to pin near short strike); -0.8 Sharpe is very bad; 42% DD is dangerous. Suggest either: switch to directional structure, add IV percentile filter, or avoid this structure in trending regimes. Do NOT suggest minor parameter tweaks as the main fix.

assertions.name: clear_negative_verdict
assertions.description: Response clearly states this is a bad result (Sharpe -0.8 is negative, return -18% is a loss)

assertions.name: regime_diagnosis
assertions.description: Response identifies likely cause as wrong regime (trending market, iron butterfly needs price to pin at strike)

assertions.name: drawdown_flagged_as_dangerous
assertions.description: Response flags 42% max drawdown as high/dangerous for a defined-risk strategy

assertions.name: suggests_regime_change_or_filter
assertions.description: Response recommends a structural change: either a different strategy type, or an entry filter that screens for range-bound conditions (e.g. IV percentile, SMA flat)

assertions.name: no_minor_tweak_only
assertions.description: Response does not only suggest small parameter tweaks (like adjusting TP from 25% to 20%); addresses the root regime mismatch

evals.prompt: Build a long straddle backtest on BTC. Buy the ATM call and put at 30 DTE. Only enter when IV is cheap — below the 35th percentile of the last 60 days. Take profit at 100%, stop loss at 50%. Max hold the full 30 days. PM margin, $150k capital.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 2 BUY ATM legs (call and put), entry.ivPctile enabled with op '<' and value 35 and window 1440 (60 days), exit.profitTarget 100, exit.stopLoss 50, exit.maxHold enabled with value 720 (30 days × 24h), PM, $150k.

assertions.name: both_legs_buy
assertions.description: Both legs have side 'BUY' — this is a long vol strategy, not short

assertions.name: both_legs_atm
assertions.description: Both legs use ATM strikes (strikeMode 'atm' or strikeParam 0.5 with strikeMode 'delta')

assertions.name: iv_entry_below_35
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.enabled is true with op '<' and value 35 — enter only when IV is cheap (bottom 35%)

assertions.name: iv_window_1440
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.window is 1440 (60 days × 24 hours)

assertions.name: profit_target_100_stop_50
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 100 and exit.stopLoss.value is 50 — long vol exit profile

assertions.name: max_hold_720
assertions.description: exit.maxHold.enabled is true and value is 720 (30 days × 24 hours)

evals.prompt: Create a BTC collar backtest: long 1 perp, buy the 25-delta put at 30 DTE for downside protection, and sell the 25-delta call at 30 DTE to offset the cost. PM margin, $150k capital. Only enter when the 8h funding rate is above 0.01% (positive carry on the perp). Exit at 30% profit or after 28 days.
evals.expected_output: JSON with exactly 3 legs: type 'perp' BUY, type 'option' BUY PUT 25Δ 30 DTE, type 'option' SELL CALL 25Δ 30 DTE. entry.fundingRate enabled op '>' value 0.01. exit.profitTarget 30. exit.maxHold 672 (28 × 24). PM, $150k.

assertions.name: three_legs_present
assertions.description: legs array contains exactly 3 entries (perp + put + call)

assertions.name: perp_buy_leg
assertions.description: One leg has type 'perp' and side 'BUY'

assertions.name: put_buy_leg
assertions.description: One leg has type 'option', side 'BUY', optionType 'PUT', strikeParam 0.25, dteTarget 30

assertions.name: call_sell_leg
assertions.description: One leg has type 'option', side 'SELL', optionType 'CALL', strikeParam 0.25, dteTarget 30

assertions.name: funding_rate_entry_filter
assertions.description: entry.fundingRate.enabled is true with op '>' and value 0.01

assertions.name: max_hold_672
assertions.description: exit.maxHold.enabled is true and value is 672 (28 days × 24 hours)

evals.prompt: Design a bullish BTC bull call spread backtest: buy the 50-delta call and sell the 25-delta call at 14 DTE. Only enter when BTC is above its 14-day SMA — I only want to run this in uptrending conditions. Exit at 75% profit or 50% loss. PM, $75k capital.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 2 CALL legs: BUY 50Δ and SELL 25Δ, both at 14 DTE. entry.sma enabled with op 'above' and period 336 (14 days × 24 hours). exit.profitTarget 75. exit.stopLoss 50. PM, $75k.

assertions.name: two_call_legs
assertions.description: Both legs have optionType 'CALL'

assertions.name: buy_50d_sell_25d
assertions.description: BUY leg has strikeParam 0.50 and SELL leg has strikeParam 0.25 (correct bull call spread structure)

assertions.name: sma_entry_filter
assertions.description: entry.sma.enabled is true with op 'above' and period 336 (14 days × 24 hours)

assertions.name: profit_target_75
assertions.description: exit.profitTarget.value is 75

assertions.name: stop_loss_50
assertions.description: exit.stopLoss.value is 50

assertions.name: pm_margin
assertions.description: marginMode is 'PM'

evals.prompt: My ETH delta-hedged short straddle backtest absolutely crushed it: Sharpe 1.9, max drawdown 7.4%, win rate 74%, total return +31% over 6 months, 22 trades. IV entry filter at IVP > 60 over 30 days. What does this tell me? Is it too good to be true? What should I do next?
evals.expected_output: Clear positive verdict (Sharpe 1.9 is excellent, drawdown 7.4% is well within target). But: caution on small sample (22 trades / 6 months), simulation approximations, and regime dependency. Actionable next steps: validate on longer data, check if regime was favourable, consider live paper trading, or hand to strategy-builder / execution for a real trade plan.

assertions.name: positive_verdict
assertions.description: Response gives a clear positive headline verdict — Sharpe 1.9 is in the excellent range (>1.5) and 7.4% drawdown is well below the 15% short-vol target

assertions.name: flags_small_sample
assertions.description: Response cautions that 22 trades over 6 months is a limited sample size and results may not be statistically reliable

assertions.name: flags_regime_dependency
assertions.description: Response notes the result may be regime-dependent (e.g., 6 months may have been unusually range-bound or high-IV) and suggests checking against a longer or different period

assertions.name: suggests_next_steps
assertions.description: Response provides concrete next steps: extend backtest window, validate with Deribit CSV, paper trade, or hand off to strategy-builder / execution planning

assertions.name: no_false_promises
assertions.description: Response does not guarantee future performance or recommend deploying maximum capital immediately

evals.prompt: Build a jade lizard backtest on SOL, 14 DTE. Sell a 25-delta put, sell the ATM call, and buy a 25-delta call as the wing. PM margin, $200k capital. Size each leg at 2% of capital. Enter weekly when IV is above the 60th percentile over 30 days. Exit at 50% profit or 2 DTE.
evals.expected_output: JSON with 3 option legs: SELL PUT 25Δ, SELL CALL ATM (50Δ), BUY CALL 25Δ. sizeMode 'pct_capital' with size 2.0 on each leg. entry.ivPctile enabled value 60 window 720. entry.frequency 168. exit.profitTarget 50. exit.dteFloor 2. SOL, PM, $200k.

assertions.name: three_option_legs
assertions.description: legs array contains exactly 3 entries, all type 'option'

assertions.name: put_sell_25d
assertions.description: One leg is SELL PUT with strikeParam 0.25

assertions.name: call_sell_atm
assertions.description: One leg is SELL CALL with ATM strike (strikeMode 'atm' or strikeParam 0.50)

assertions.name: call_buy_25d
assertions.description: One leg is BUY CALL with strikeParam 0.25 (the call spread wing)

assertions.name: pct_capital_sizing
assertions.description: At least one leg uses sizeMode 'pct_capital' with size 2.0

assertions.name: iv_entry_60_window_720
assertions.description: entry.ivPctile.enabled is true with value 60 and window 720

assertions.name: dte_floor_2
assertions.description: exit.dteFloor.enabled is true and value is 2
